name: Fresh Pasta with Tomatoes and Calamari
recipeIngredient: 4 Italian Roma tomatoes, split, roasted and julienned
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the semolina and salt together.
2. On a large work surface, form a mound with the semolina and create a "well" in the center of the mound.
3. In a small bowl, beat the eggs, squid ink, and oil together until smooth. Pour this mixture into the well.
4. Working from the inside of the well out, gradually work the semolina into the eggs, using a fork, until all the flour is incorporated.
5. Wrap with plastic wrap and allow to rest in the refrigerator for 30 to 40 minutes.
6. Remove from the refrigerator and let it temper for 5 minutes.
7. Flour a clean surface and turn out the dough onto it. Roll out the dough to about 1/2 inch thick.
8. Cut the dough into thirds. Using a pasta machine, process one piece of the dough through the wide cutters.
9. Lightly toss the cut pasta in flour to prevent sticking. Process the remaining pieces through the pasta machine.
10. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
11. In a hot sauté pan, add the heavy cream. Reduce over high heat until halved, about 5 minutes.
12. Stir in the grated Parmesan and Romano cheeses and season with salt and pepper. Continue to simmer until the sauce coats the back of a spoon, about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from the heat and set aside.
13. Drop the pasta into the boiling water and cook for 3 minutes, or until al dente. Drain the pasta and transfer to the cream sauce.
14. Add the tomatoes, green onions, and fried calamari to the pasta/cream mixture. Toss to combine all ingredients.
15. Divide the pasta among four bowls. Garnish with chopped parsley and grated cheese.
This recipe yields 1 pound of pasta or 4 main-dish servings.
